mnemonics: CIA Exam Day: Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability are the core principles to remember for the exam day.
keyPoints: Understand the fundamental principles of security: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability (CIA Triad)
Be able to differentiate between symmetric and asymmetric encryption algorithms and their use cases
Know common network attacks (e.g., DDoS, Phishing, Man-in-the-Middle) and their countermeasures
commonMistakes: Confusing authentication (proving identity) with authorization (granting access rights)
Mixing up the functions of a firewall (packet filtering, stateful inspection) with an IDS/IPS (threat detection/prevention)
Using the terms 'hacking' and 'cracking' interchangeably, when cracking implies malicious intent
